methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $300M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Cemvita Factory is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Moji Karimi, currently valued at $120M post-money on $30M total raised. The valuation analysis triangulates against a peer set of 5 private companies in the same sector and stage, alongside 1 relevant public comparable (LNZA), representing the core comparable universe for frontier-energy technology at this funding stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $228M based on the current $120M post-money. Peer-median analysis across five climate and energy companies at Series B stage—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—produces a median implied valuation of $400M, though the range spans $300M to $5.0B, reflecting material dispersion in early-stage climate tech valuations.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $194M to $460M, with a median of $314M. The current $120M valuation sits below this range, suggesting potential upside to next-stage funding. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion (3.3x spread from min to median), the outsized influence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems as an outlier, and sector-specific capital allocation volatility in climate tech. Stage-progression and peer analysis are both applicable but yield results sufficiently divergent to warrant cautious interpretation pending additional market signals.
